explanation.title: Writing Meaningful HTML with Semantic Elements
explanation.content:
# What is Semantic HTML? Semantic HTML means using elements that describe the **meaning** of their content, not just its visual appearance. Compare: ```html
...
...
...
...
...
...
``` Both render identically in a browser. But semantic HTML gives three major benefits: 1. **Accessibility** — screen readers use semantic elements to help blind users navigate: `
` means they can jump to the navigation, `
` means they can skip to the main content 2. **SEO** — search engines use `

`, `
`, `
` to understand what the page is about and rank it accordingly 3. **Maintainability** — other developers (and your future self) understand the structure immediately ## The Key Semantic Elements | Element | Meaning | Replaces | |---|---|---| | `
` | Page or section header | `
` | | `
` | Navigation links | `
` | | `
` | Primary page content (use once) | `
` | | `
` | Self-contained content (blog post, card) | `
` | | `
` | Thematic grouping of content | `
` | | `
` | Related but secondary content | `
` | | `
` | Page or section footer | `
` | | `

codeExample.title: Semantic Blog Article Page
codeExample.code:
CodeGuru AI
Home Courses Blog

Getting Started with Python in 2025

By Rahul Sharma · 15 January 2025 · 8 min read

Why Python?

Python is the most beginner-friendly programming language and the top choice for AI, data science, and web development.

Pro Tip: Start with the official Python tutorial at python.org before moving to third-party resources.

Installing Python

Visit python.org/downloads and click the yellow button. Always choose Python 3, never Python 2.

codeExample.explanation:
- `position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10` on `header` — the nav sticks to the top as the user scrolls
- `clamp(24px, 5vw, 44px)` on `h1` — fluid font size; scales between 24px and 44px with the viewport
- Anchor links like `href="#projects"` jump to the element with `id="projects"` on the same page
- `flex: 0 0 calc(33.333% - 14px)` — three-column cards with 20px gap compensation
- Mobile breakpoints convert three columns to two at 768px and one at 480px

exercise.title: Convert Divs to Semantic Elements
exercise.instructions:
The starter code uses div elements with id attributes for all structure. Replace each div with the appropriate semantic element: div#header becomes header, div#nav becomes nav, div#main becomes main, div#article becomes article, div#sidebar becomes aside, and div#footer becomes footer. Keep all existing CSS classes and styles unchanged. Only change the HTML tag names.

Expected: six semantic elements replacing six div elements.
exercise.starterCode:
My Site
HomeAbout

Welcome

Main content here.

Links

Related content.

© 2025 My Site
exercise.solutionCode:
My Site
HomeAbout

Welcome

Main content here.

Links

Related content.

© 2025 My Site
hints: 💡 Replace <div id="header"> with <header id="header"> — keep the id so the CSS still targets it.
💡 Replace <div id="nav"> with <nav id="nav">, div#sidebar with <aside id="sidebar">, div#article with <article id="article">.
💡 Replace the outermost div#main with <main id="main"> and div#footer with <footer id="footer">.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using semantic HTML elements over generic divs?
options: Semantic elements load faster than divs
Semantic elements give meaning to content, helping screen readers and search engines
Semantic elements cannot be styled with CSS
Semantic elements automatically add visual styling
questions.explanation: Semantic elements communicate the role of their content to browsers, screen readers, and search engines. A nav element tells assistive technology 'this is the navigation', enabling keyboard shortcuts for blind users. Search engines use article and main to understand page content better. Divs convey no meaning — they are purely structural containers.

questions.question: How many main elements should a well-structured HTML page contain?
options: Exactly one — it represents the single primary content area
As many as needed for layout
questions.explanation: The main element should appear exactly once per page and contain the primary unique content of that page. Screen readers present main as a landmark that users can jump to directly. Having multiple main elements confuses both users and validators. Headers, footers, navigation, and sidebars all go outside main.
